text.title: Stripe Webhook Handler Pattern Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Stripe Webhook Handler Pattern. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint deployed without signature check 'just for testing'; attacker injects fake events.
antipattern.root-cause: Verification gated behind a feature flag that never flipped on.
antipattern.fix: Restore SDK verification; rotate webhook secret; audit recent events for forged ones.

antipattern.symptom: Stripe retries lead to double-charging customers; ops spend a week reconciling refunds.
antipattern.fix: Add unique index on event_id; refund duplicates; document remediation.

antipattern.symptom: Handler does 15s of inline work; Stripe retries; 5x duplicate fulfilment.
antipattern.fix: Move heavy work to queue (SQS or similar); return 2xx immediately after dedup write.

antipattern.symptom: Failed event silently dropped; customer did not get provisioning.
antipattern.root-cause: No DLQ; bare 5xx and Stripe gave up after 3 days.
antipattern.fix: Add DLQ; manual review SLA; re-process safely from the dead-letter.
